concerns.text: SC-004 states: 'The validity of the dataset is measured by ensuring [deferred] of the 200 sampled problems contain the required variables...' The use of '[deferred]' here renders the criterion unmeasurable. A success criterion must define a specific threshold (e.g., '100%' or '≥99%') or a reproducible procedure to verify validity. 'Ensuring [deferred]' is not a testable condition.

SC-005 states: '...total runtime... does not exceed 6 hours on a standard CPU-only runner.' The term 'standard CPU-only runner' is not a verifiable specification. Different CI runners (e.g., GitHub Actions 'ubuntu-latest' vs. 'ubuntu-20.04', or varying CPU generations) have different performance characteristics. Without specifying the exact runner type or a hardware baseline, this threshold cannot be deterministically tested.

FR-001 requires sampling a 'representative subset' to ensure 'statistical power'. While the Assumptions section mentions a sample size of 200, the requirement itself lacks a deterministic check for 'representativeness'. A verifiable FR should specify the sampling method (e.g., 'System MUST randomly sample 200 problems') rather than the subjective quality 'representative'.
